zh.lifeSummary: 當Jane Clarke於1160年出生在英國時，她的父親Clarke21歲，母親Wife Clarke19歲。她和John Neily至少有1個女兒。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jane Clarke于1160年出生在英国时，她的父亲Clarke21岁，母亲Wife Clarke19岁。她和John Neily至少有1个女儿。

nameMeanings.name: Clarke
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish: variant of   Clark  .

History: This name was brought independently to New England by many bearers from the 17th century onward. Nicholas Clarke was one of the founders of Hartford, CT (coming from Cambridge, MA with Thomas Hooker) in 1635.

variants.name: Clark
meanings: English: from Middle English clerk, clark ‘clerk, cleric, writer’ (Old French clerc; see Clerc ). The original sense was ‘man in a religious order, cleric, clergyman’. As all writing and secretarial work in medieval Christian Europe was normally done by members of the clergy, the term clerk came to mean ‘scholar, secretary, recorder, or penman’ as well as ‘cleric’. As a surname, it was particularly common for one who had taken only minor holy orders. In medieval Christian Europe, clergy in minor orders were permitted to marry and so found families; thus the surname could become established.
Irish (Westmeath, Mayo): in Ireland the English surname was frequently adopted, partly by translation for Ó Cléirigh; see Cleary .
Americanized form of Dutch   De Klerk   or Flemish   De Clerck   or of variants of these names, and possibly also of French   Clerc  . Compare   Clerk   2 and   De Clark  .

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Jane Clarke was born in 1160, in England, her father, Clarke, was 21 and her mother, Wife Clarke, was 19. She had at least 1 daughter with John Neily.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jane Clarke est née en 1160, à Angleterre, son père, Clarke, avait 21 ans et sa mère, Wife Clarke, avait 19 ans. Elle a eu au moins 1 fille avec John Neily.

de.lifeSummary: Als Jane Clarke 1160 in England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Clarke 21 und ihre Mutter Wife Clarke 19. Sie hatte mit John Neily mindestens 1 Tochter.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jane Clarke è nata il nel 1160 nel England, sua padre, Clarke, aveva 21 anni e sua madre, Wife Clarke, aveva 19 anni. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlia con John Neily.

ja.lifeSummary: Jane Clarkeが1160年にイギリスで生まれた時，父Clarkeは21歳，母Wife Clarkeは19歳であった。彼女はJohn Neilyとの間に少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。

ko.lifeSummary: Jane Clarke(이)가 1160년 영국에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Clarke(은)는 21세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Wife Clarke(은)는 19세였다. 그녀는 John Neily(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jane Clarke nasceu em 1160, em Inglaterra, seu pai, Clarke, tinha 21 anos e sua mãe, Wife Clarke, tinha 19 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filha com John Neily.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jane Clarke родилась в 1160 г. в местности Англия, её отцу по имени Clarke было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Wife Clarke, 19. У неё была по крайней мере 1 дочь от John Neily.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Jane Clarke nació en 1160, en Inglaterra, su papá, Clarke, tenía 21 años y su mamá, Wife Clarke, tenía 19 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija con John Neily.
